items.label: Frequency Counter Lookup Key Error
items.notes: Student checks index instead of value inside hash maps, or uses variable references incorrectly as keys.

items.label: Wrong Pointer Increment/Decrement
items.notes: Student increments right index instead of decrementing, or modifies bounds incorrectly based on condition.

items.label: Sliding Window Off-by-One Bounds Error
items.notes: Happens when calculating subarray size as right - left instead of right - left + 1, or failing to shrink window dynamically.

items.label: Null Pointer Dereference
symptoms: segmentation fault or runtime null error
items.notes: Student calls curr.next.next without verifying that curr.next is not null.

items.label: Cycle Check Pointer Step Error
symptoms: fast pointer bypasses slow pointer without meeting
null pointer exception on fast step
items.notes: Student forgets to move fast pointer by two steps or doesn't check fast.next for null before stepping.

items.label: Missing Recursion Base Case
items.notes: The recursion function continues executing indefinitely because the base case condition is missing or never reached.

items.label: Incorrect Traversal Ordering Node Process
items.notes: Student confuses preorder processing (parent first) with postorder processing (children first).

items.label: Missing Visited Set in Graph Traversal
symptoms: infinite loop stuck on circular graph paths
items.notes: Unlike trees, graphs have cycles. Forgetting to mark nodes as visited leads to recursive cycle loops.

items.label: Forgetting to Undo Decision State Modification
symptoms: output contains elements from invalid search branches
items.notes: Student appends a candidate to the path list but forgets to pop/remove it after the recursive call returns.

items.label: Cyclic Dependency Deadlock Failure
items.notes: Topological sort is only possible in DAGs. If the graph contains cycles, the system must detect it and return an empty list.
